steps.title: Find the gesture (pencil)
steps.description: Lightly sketch a single curving line for the bird's spine — starting low at the tail, rising up through the body, and arching forward into where the head will hang. Pelicans hunch; let yours hunch too.

steps.title: Block the body and head (pencil)
steps.description: Still in pencil, sketch a large oval for the body around the lower part of your gesture, and a smaller oval for the head at the top of the arc. Keep them clearly separate — you'll connect them with a neck in a moment.

steps.title: Block the bill and pouch (pencil)
steps.description: Sketch a long flattened wedge extending forward from the head — the upper bill. Beneath it, sketch a sagging curve for the pouch that hangs from the lower bill. The bill is roughly as long as the head is tall.

steps.title: Mark the legs and feet (pencil)
steps.description: Sketch two short vertical lines beneath the body for the legs, and two small triangles at the bottom for the webbed feet. The legs are stubby — pelicans don't have a lot of leg to show.

steps.title: Switch to ink and trace the silhouette
steps.description: Switch to ink. Trace the outline of the body, neck, head, bill, and pouch in one mostly continuous pass. Where the bill meets the head and where the pouch sags below, let your lines land cleanly at the junctions.

steps.title: Ink the wing and tail (ink)
steps.description: Still in ink, draw a single long curve along the body to suggest the folded wing tucked against the back. Add a short angled line at the rear for the tail tip.

steps.title: Suggest the feathers and eye (ink)
steps.description: Add a tiny dot for the eye near the top of the head, and a small scatter of short marks along the wing edge to suggest feathers. Don't render every feather — a few well-placed strokes carry the texture.
